title: Fellow Opus Conical Burr Coffee Grinder - All Purpose Electric - Espresso Grinder with 41 Settings for Drip, French Press, & Cold Brew - Matte Black
description:
About this item
OPUS CONICAL BURR GRINDER - Grinds true espresso, while also covering the full range of precise grind sizes for from pour-over to cold brew. 41+ easily adjustable settings, plus expanded range and granularity using the inner ring.
CONSISTENCY YOU CAN TASTE - A 6-blade 40 mm conical burr set and powerful motor with 6Nm of torque deliver outstanding consistency and flavor across all brewing styles. Just ask the Certified Q Graders (the master sommeliers of coffee).
ALL-PURPOSE, ALL THE TIME- Use the volumetric dosing lid to measure beans for up to 12 cups of brewed coffee, pop the load bin lid for a grind guide so you’re never lost, make precision adjustments using the inner ring, and keep your settings in check with unique no-tool calibration.
MESS-FREE MORNINGS - Drop-down design from load bin to catch radically reduces grind retention, while the anti-static technology and spouted catch keeps your countertop spotless.
FELLOW: Experts in coffee with a love for product design, we at Fellow believe coffee is a journey. We are dedicated to melding beautiful design with flawless functionality to help aficionados and beginners alike find the fun in the brewing process.
reviews_5s:
I was looking for a high-quality coffee grinder that offered versatility and precision, and the Fellow Opus Conical Burr Coffee Grinder exceeded all my expectations!

The matte black design is sleek and fits perfectly in my modern kitchen. Additionally, the build is sturdy and gives off a premium quality feel.

What really stands out are the 41 grind settings. They allow you to adjust the grind for any brewing method, from espresso to French press and cold brew. I’ve tested several settings and was impressed by the consistency of the grind, which makes a huge difference in the coffee’s flavor.

Another highlight is how quiet it is compared to other electric grinders I’ve used. The interface is intuitive, making it very easy to switch between settings, and cleaning is also super simple.I couldn’t be more thrilled with the Fellow Opus Conical Burr Coffee Grinder—it has transformed my coffee routine! As a coffee enthusiast, I’ve gone through a few grinders, but the Fellow Opus truly stands out with its incredible design, precision, and consistency. From the moment I took it out of the box, I was impressed by its sleek, modern look. It feels sturdy and well-built, with every detail thoughtfully designed.

One of my favorite features is the 40+ grind settings, which makes it super versatile for any brewing method. Whether I’m in the mood for a rich espresso, a balanced pour-over, or a bold French press, the Fellow Opus handles it all. The conical burrs ensure a uniform grind, which I can taste in the cup—the flavors are more vibrant, and the aromas more pronounced than with my previous grinders. It’s amazing how this one tool brings out the best in every coffee bean, highlighting notes I didn’t even know were there.

The grinder’s functionality is just as impressive. The dial for adjusting the grind size is easy to use, and switching between settings is seamless. Plus, it’s incredibly quiet, which is a game-changer for early mornings when I don’t want to wake up the whole house! I’ve had other grinders that sounded like power tools, but the Fellow Opus operates with a gentle hum that’s easy on the ears.

Another aspect that I appreciate is the Fellow Opus’s ease of cleaning. Taking it apart to clean is straightforward, and it’s simple to keep it in pristine condition without much effort. This is huge because consistent maintenance keeps the grind quality high over time.

Overall, I’m beyond happy with this grinder it’s worth every penny. It’s obvious that Fellow put a lot of thought into designing something that is not only aesthetically pleasing but also exceptionally functional. The Opus has become an essential part of my kitchen, and it’s completely elevated my coffee experience. I’d recommend it to any coffee lover looking for top-notch quality, precision, and a grinder that makes each cup a joy to brew.I love the Fellow Opus Coffee Grinder! It’s super easy to use, 41 grind settings make it really good for any kind of coffee, I’ve tried espresso and cold brew so far. The grinder is fast, quiet, and gives consistent results every time. I also like how it looks—modern and really nice, it fits perfectly in my kitchen. If you want great coffee at home, this grinder is worth every penny!The Fellow Opus Conical Burr Coffee Grinder delivers precise, consistent grinds for every brew method, from espresso to French press. Its sleek design complements any kitchen, while the intuitive controls make it easy to dial in the perfect grind size. With exceptional build quality and quiet operation, it’s a reliable tool for coffee lovers seeking professional-level results at home. The Opus brings out the full flavor of every bean with ease.I have honestly wanted to buy a great, high-quality coffee grinder for a while now, and I am so glad we took the leap!

The Fellow Opus Conical Burr Coffee Grinder is extremely consistent, has over 40 grind settings (WOW), and is user-friendly to those of us who are clueless on coffee making but love coffee and want to make our own at home!

The grinder can be used easily after unpacking (it is practically put together ready to use out of the box), and it is very functional with easy to understand and clear instructions included. I never truly knew the coffee science prior to finding the Fellow brand, but I love that I am able to try anything from espresso to French Press! The conical burrs, the dial for adjustments to the grind size, and every detail in between make this a must have for my daily coffee routine/ritual. I love that this is VERY quiet, too! I have tried out other coffee grinders when traveling, and this one is the BEST, hands down no questions asked.

I was worried about how easy (or hard) cleaning this coffee grinder would be. As someone who is inexperienced, I found it extremely easy! Plus, cleaning the grinder regularly keeps it in great shape for maximum quality grinding to the setting of your choice every single time.

All in all, I highly recommend the Fellow Opus Conical Burr Coffee Grinder to anyone who loves coffee, wants to develop an at-home (or even office) routine, needs a gift for a coffee lover, etc. It’s worth the price. It’s worth the ease of use. It is a must have in our home at our coffee bar! We are obsessed with our investment. Yay Fellow!!!I recently invested in the Fellow Opus Conical Burr Grinder, and I can confidently say it’s been a game-changer for my coffee experience! As someone who loves a well-crafted cup, I’m always on the lookout for high-quality tools, and the Opus delivers on nearly every front.

Pros:
• Consistent Grind Quality: The Opus offers a level of precision that’s rare in this price range. From fine espresso grounds to coarse French press, the grind consistency is impressive. I noticed an immediate difference in the taste and aroma of my coffee, as every bean is ground uniformly.
• Quiet Operation: I’m blown away by how quiet this grinder is! Compared to my previous grinder, the Opus is noticeably less noisy, making it ideal for early mornings when I don’t want to wake the whole household.
• Sleek, Compact Design: The design is modern and compact, with that signature Fellow aesthetic that looks great on my countertop. It’s also easy to clean and maintain, with a sturdy, minimalist build that feels premium.
• Intuitive Interface: Using the Opus is a breeze. The dial settings are clear, and I love the option to adjust grind size so quickly, which is especially handy when switching between brew methods.

Cons:
• Small Hopper Size: While the grinder’s compact design is a plus, the hopper is a bit on the small side. If you’re brewing for a crowd or making multiple pots, you may need to refill more often.
• Slightly Pricey: The Opus isn’t cheap, especially compared to some other grinders on the market. However, I believe it’s worth every penny if you’re serious about your coffee. That said, if you’re more of a casual coffee drinker, it might feel like a bit of an investment.
• Static Buildup: Occasionally, there’s a bit of static in the grounds container, which can cause some coffee to stick to the sides. It’s not a major issue, but a little surprising given the grinder’s otherwise top-notch performance.

Final Thoughts:
Overall, the Fellow Opus Conical Grinder has elevated my coffee routine and given me a new appreciation for freshly ground beans. The pros far outweigh the minor cons, and I’m genuinely excited to use it every morning. If you’re a coffee enthusiast looking to upgrade your setup, this grinder is a worthy investment that combines form, function, and quality into one impressive package. Highly recommended!The Fellow Burr Coffee Grinder is a sleek and precise tool, perfect for coffee enthusiasts seeking consistent results. Its minimalist design, sturdy stainless steel burrs, and magnetic catch cup make it both functional and stylish. The stepless adjustment dial offers exceptional control with over 30 grind settings, accommodating methods from espresso to French press.
Grind consistency is excellent, ensuring balanced flavors, and its low retention design keeps grounds fresh. Maintenance is simple, with removable burrs and minimal static cling. While the price is steep and the capacity is modest, the grinder’s premium build and performance make it worth the investment for serious coffee lovers.The Fellow Opus Conical Burr Coffee Grinder is a total game-changer for any coffee enthusiast. Right out of the box, it impresses with its sleek matte black finish that looks stunning on any countertop. But the real magic happens when you put it to use.

With 41 grind settings, this grinder caters to every brewing style—whether you’re into rich espressos, smooth drip coffee, or bold cold brew. Adjusting the grind is super easy, and the consistency is flawless every time, giving your coffee the perfect balance of flavor and aroma.

It’s also surprisingly quiet, which is a bonus for early risers who don't want to wake the house. The compact design makes it convenient to store, but honestly, you’ll want to leave it out just because it looks so cool.

The Fellow Opus Grinder proves that you don’t need to be a barista to enjoy coffee-shop-quality drinks at home. If you’re serious about coffee, this is a must-have addition to your kitchen. Five stars!I love drip coffee - 6 or 7 cups a day at my office and home!
I have Fellow Gen 2 Ode at home, and really enjoy it so that I bought Opus at my office this time.
This is a little cheaper model, but works well and go beyond my expectation.
I love this.
A little pricy, but worthy every penny!I absolutely love my Fellow coffee grinder! From the moment I unboxed it, I could tell it was designed with care and attention to detail. The sleek, modern look fits perfectly in my kitchen, and it feels sturdy and well-built. The grind settings are incredibly precise, and I’ve been able to switch effortlessly between espresso and French press without any issues. What really stands out is how quiet it is compared to other grinders I’ve used, and it doesn’t overheat the beans, which keeps the flavor intact. It’s a bit of an investment, but for me, the consistent performance and beautiful design make it totally worth it.
reviews_1s:
I've used the same Baratza Virtuoso grinder for over a decade. Hundreds of pounds of coffee have been ground with very little issues and all were very easy to fix because it's designed to be serviceable. It has its shortcomings, grind consistency being one of them, but you can work around it, especially since it generally will allow some coffee through that's slightly larger then desired. Not a dealbreaker. But when it started getting loud I knew it needed some parts replaced but I decided to "upgrade" my grinder instead. Here's where the Opus comes in.

Researching options I kept reading that the more expensive Fellow Ode grinder was very prone to clogging and unclogging things was a lot of work. Many said it happened every couple weeks. Oddly I wasn't seeing as many complaining about the Opus so I opted for this. When first setup it seemed to be a great looking grinder but that was short lived.

I measured out 50g of medium roast beans for a partial pot of electric coffee maker coffee, set the grinder to 8 which is in the middle of where they recommend per the inside of the grinder lid (props to them for the neat chart), and told it to grind. . . Grinder turned on but the beans weren't feeding down to the burrs. . . Once I helped them along it started to grind. When I removed the catch bin it was immediately obvious the coffee was ground too fine. I tried brewing it anyways and the basket ran over, leaving grounds in the coffee. Let's try this again.

I pulled the lid off the top of the grinder and the bean load bin had chafe all over in it. The top lid creates an air tight(ish) seal when you slide it on so upon removal it pulled the lightweight chafe into the bin. Cleaned it out, poured beans in, adjusted the grind to 9 which is the coarsest they recommend for my coffee method, and put the lid back on. But now the air tight lid forced trapped coffee grounds out the bottom of the grinder, all over the place. . . Right in their description they say "mess-free mornings" and "anti-static technology" yet I'm not seeing either here. Cleaned things up and ground the coffee beans and immediately noticed there barely a difference in the grind, still being much too fine.

Tried again with a grind setting of 10, and again at 11. Even at 11 (the coarsest it goes) the grind was too fine for the brew method even though this is the setting for cold brew, what should be a very coarse grind. And I didn't give up here. I proceeded to use the grinder for a week, trying to figure out what's going on, and as time went by I couldn't get a good grind but did notice the grind time was getting longer and longer, and it seemed as though the grind was getting finer. . . It was clogging, just like Ode reviews complained about. It was taking 4 minutes to grind 50g of medium roast beans- I can hand grind faster than that. We're done with this grinder.

Before boxing it back up I ran a little test for others to see what I'm dealing with. In the pictures the left dish is a grind setting of 5, the middle is 7.5, and the right is 10. Very little difference, especially considering 5 is the middle grind setting but was so fine that it's espresso fineness, and 10 is the coarse cold brew setting.

All that being said, avoid this grinder. Other reviewers give it praise but that's not what we saw. The Encore that replaced this (for less money) worked right out of the box, with the grinder set to the recommended setting and we finally had a good pot of electric brewer coffee.It seemed to work great for about a week. I made pour over 3 times, by the forth time it just spun and spun with no grinding. I watched all of their youtube videos on how to clean and how to fix to no avail. Also what they don’t tell you is what a mess this thing creates, especially if it stops working without making it through all the deposited beans. It’s virtually impossible to get them out without coffee going everywhere. Also when removing the bur part or however you spell it the flimsy metal handle will fall out if it’s even the slightest bit jammed and then you have to go digging through your half grinded coffee for a part you need to further complete the ineffective cleaning. Of course after working 3 times and paying 200$ or whatever for this thing I assumed it would continue to work but sure thing as soon I throw out the box, it breaks and now I’m stuck unable to return this hunk of garbage, I honestly might take it outside and smash it into pieces with a hammer just to let the frustration out. I suggest spending a little more or a little less money so you either get something that works or don’t get frustrated at it breaking within 8 minutes of actual usage time. Well if you are unfortunate enough to acquire this thing Godspeed and good luck. Now just waiting for this electronic kettle to break and I’ll go in for a whole new settup. I hope for the companies sake this is planned obsolescence and not manufacturing incompetence.This worked great for a few weeks. Sure the hopper is small, and the 30 second timer takes some getting used to, but it ground consistent and it is quiet. It looks cool too.

THEN, it started retaining coffee. At first it was a little bit. And even though it was a little bit, that little bit would wait until the bottom cup was removed to drop out of the machine.

No amount of pumping, shaking or tapping can get that coffee out. It simply waits until the most inopportune moment and just falls out in a neat little pile, as soon as there’s nothing underneath to catch it. It was practically mocking me.

This little pile of retained coffee started getting larger and larger and starts to spread further and further on my countertop.

Time to clean. The design makes it IMPOSSIBLE to clean. I had to stick a toothpick up the coffee shoot and wiggle all the coffee out. And oh boy, there’s a lot of coffee stuck in there. Theres no way to tell how much coffee this thing retains because it’s impossible to see up the coffee shoot. But it retains a lot! I could brew a whole pot with the coffee that was stuck in this grinder.

Eventually, no matter how meticulously I clean, after a few grinds, absolutely nothing comes out and I have to awkwardly wiggle out a pot’s worth of coffee with a toothpick. Literally after a few grinds, the dose gets too small to use. Within a week, barely any coffee comes out when it’s supposed to, and my counter becomes covered in a thin layer of coffee grounds.

I wish this thing worked as well as it did when I first got it. For the price, the performance was great, until it became completely useless.

I’m glad I kept the box. I’m returning it.I got the grinder. It worked right the first time. As I go to make my 2nd cup of coffee of the day in my pour over, which is the same brand. It just doesn’t work. I teared apart, clean out the grind it back in still nothing. Should I go back onto Amazon This is the second product that I’ve gotten from Amazon that does not say it’s from the second party seller. So then when I go to do my Return of a product that’s not working, it tells me I might get my refund if all parts are attached, which which I used it one time. 😳 then I have to have a pick up By UPS I can’t even put it as a drop off at a drop UPS. I sent it back on November 2. We’re on November 19. They still have not received My package to even start a refund. I’m really getting tired of Amazon not marking that. It is a third-party seller or that it is coming from China to get overturn is almost impossible.Grinder worked great for first 3 months. I had it dialed in perfectly for my espresso. 1.75 on outer ring and -1 or 0 on inner ring. Then all of a sudden yesterday it seems as though the grinder adjustment settings are completely different. If I grind on my usual setting the beans don't actually get ground like usual. They sort of just bounce around and take a long time to get ground. Also the grounds produced at 1.75 now are too fine and choke my espresso machine. To not choke my espresso machine I now have to go all the way to 2.75 or 3 on the outer ring. That is way... Way... Way... outside the espresso range recommended on the Opus lid, which was once accurate. The grinder technically still grinds but I am have all kinds of issues dialing in now. I love this grinder and was very satisfied with it for the 3 months that it worked. For a machine that is nearly $200, I am a bit disappointed.

Update Jan 19th 2024: It happened again! Reducing to 1 star.
Have had my Opus dialed in with the same coffee for three weeks at 2.5 setting. Today I go to pull a shot and my machine chokes. I end up have to pull 4 consecutive shots to determine it is grinding too fine at 2.5 all of a sudden. I end up having to go all the way up to 3.5 before my espresso machine no longer chokes. This is in the Aero press range! At only 5 months old for a $200 grind this product is a 100% DO NOT BUY. Fellow support has only responded with saying that these effects are from "burr seasons" and are "normal".
Do yourself a favor and go buy a Baratza Encore ESP for the same money.Coffee grinders are typically not convenient. There's always cleanup, maintenance, and they can get quite messy. But this Fellow Opus is the worst I've used. If you try to grind 8 oz of beans, be prepared to stop halfway to take the entire unit apart to give it a deep clean as it will get jammed. You'll end up wasting some grinds too as you jam a straw or chop stick into the innards to break free the caked on grounds.

Also there are settings for 30s, 60s, 90s, and 2 min. Which is funny as 90s equates to a full catch chamber. I didn't see anything in the manual advising to not use the 2 min. My advice is do not since 2 minutes is far longer than what that little bin will hold. In fact, 90s is also going to fill that bin. Other grinders I've used don't have timer settings which go beyond the limitations of the bin. Because why would they?

I thought it was user error the first time. But after learning it's limitations and adjusting it's still a hot mess. Disappointing product (also disappointed at ATK for the recco).I regret this purchase. Static electricity is created during the grinding process…which make a complete mess. The coffee dust/shavings clings to everything plastic. It’s difficult to pour all the grounds out of the collection cup because of the static (the grounds stick to the sides of the cup. I have followed the manufacturer instructions on cleaning to eliminate static… nothing has helped at all. I spend more time cleaning up after grinding than I do enjoying coffee. It’s too expensive of a purchase to throw it in the trash, unfortunately!

For some reason, maybe it’s me, the coffee grounds, the grounds collection cup and basically the entire outside of the coffee makeAfter a few weeks of having this grinder back in 2023, the grinder refused to stay on and grind any amount of beans. Then weeks with product support telling me I needed to clean the grinder after several videos of me showing a completely spotless grinder and even videos of the grinder stalling without beans or a burr, I demanded a refund or a new unit. Finally they replaced my unit. Weeks after getting a second unit, the same issues were occurring forcing me to spend way too much time trying to make a cup of coffee on top of cleaning the HUGE mess this grinder make.

Fast forward a year, this grinder continues to waste counter space barely being used because every time my wife and I need to use it we want to throw it against the wall. The last straw was tonight and this POS is going in the garbage where it belongs. This is one of the worst products I have ever purchased.

Save your time and money by chewing coffee beans in your mouth. It will be a more pleasant experience than using this grinder or talking to product support.After carefully researching and comparing my grinder options, I decided to purchase the Fellow Opus. Initially, I was thrilled with its performance—it delivered great results during the first few weeks. However, after a few months, retention issues began to surface. At first, it was just a small amount of coffee grounds that could be managed with meticulous cleaning. But over time, the problem escalated. The grounds started jamming consistently, and eventually, the grinder became completely unusable.

I haven’t even owned the Fellow Opus for a year, and it’s already broken. I truly regret this purchase.As a coffee nerd, I had high hopes for this aesthetically-pleasing brand. I admit that I am extremely disappointed. The grinder stopped working after the second use. It doesn’t get much more high maintenance than having to fully deconstruct your machine every two uses for proper functioning.

I hope this brand works on its build quality. I definitely do not recommend this grinder and I would recommend going with more established brands until this manufacturer picks up its build quality.
